architecture_rules.workspace_layout: All Rust crates under crates/<name>/. Workspace root Cargo.toml owns lints + dependency pins. No crate adds its own lints — inherit from workspace.lints.
architecture_rules.crate_naming: All AIOS crates prefixed with aios- (aios-action, aios-evidence, aios-policy, aios-capability-runtime, aios-fs, aios-session).
architecture_rules.schema_first: Each crate that exposes a wire surface MUST have a corresponding proto3 schema in its proto/ subdirectory, matching the rev.2 sub-spec's Appendix A IDL. Code generation via tonic-build at compile time.
architecture_rules.no_unwrap: Workspace lint forbids .unwrap(), .expect(), panic!(), todo!(), unimplemented!(). Use Result<T, E> + thiserror for all error paths.
architecture_rules.no_unsafe: Workspace lint forbids unsafe code. Exceptions require explicit DEC entry.

spec_references.design_decisions: 002.AI-OS.NET--SPECREV.2/02_design_decisions.md (DEC log, newest at top of post-foundation block — DEC-056 currently)

constitutional_invariants_to_uphold: INV-006 Web UI localhost-only by default
INV-009 Approvals bound to one request and expire
INV-011 Cross-group access forbidden by default
INV-012 Recovery required for system mutation
INV-013 AI cannot perform system admin
INV-016 AI cannot grade its own work
INV-018 Vault never leaks raw secrets
INV-019..024 Visual identity / chrome / GPU constitutional

implementation_status_taxonomy.rule: Never claim REAL without explicit evidence at the required grade per S6.1 §10 / S6.2 §6

reusable_lessons.rfc8785_jcs_adoption: RFC 8785 (JSON Canonicalization Scheme) in Rust is a one-dep adoption: `serde_jcs = "0.1"` (Governor-approved in T-006). The crate exposes `serde_jcs::to_string<T: Serialize>(value: &T) -> serde_json::Result<String>`; drop-in replacement for an inline `serde_json::to_string` after a recursive key-sort pass. Pulls in `ryu-js` (the ECMA-262 ryu fork) for §3.2.2.3 number normalization. The deficit being closed: under plain `serde_json::to_string`, `1.5e2` and `150.0` (numerically equal IEEE-754 doubles) emit as different strings and therefore hash to different `request_hash` values — which silently breaks idempotency_key safe-retry. Pin this via a golden test that constructs two requests with `serde_json::Number::from_f64(1.5e2)` vs literal `150.0` and asserts `request_hash` equality.
reusable_lessons.rust_integration_test_layout: Cargo treats every `crates/<crate>/tests/*.rs` as an independent integration target — no Cargo.toml entry needed. Shared helpers go in `tests/common/mod.rs` and are included from each target via `mod common;` (a sub-mod under tests/). Each target compiles to its own binary, so `dead_code` warnings fire when a target uses a subset of helpers — use `#![allow(dead_code)]` at the module level with a `reason = "..."` attribute under `clippy::allow_attributes_without_reason`. Fixture files live in `tests/fixtures/*.json`; resolve absolute paths via `PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")).join("tests/fixtures")` rather than relying on cwd.
reusable_lessons.transparent_serde_id_newtypes_skip_valid: An ID newtype declared `#[serde(transparent)]` over a `String` will accept ANY string in JSON deserialization — its `parse()` validator does NOT run. This is the correct Wave-11 posture (validation belongs at construction-time, not at the wire), but it means golden fixtures can carry opaque test-only id strings (`act_01HQZX1234PARENT00000ABCDE`) without needing real ULIDs.
reusable_lessons.clippy_type_complexity_in_tests: `[(fn(&mut T), U); N]` arrays of function pointers trip `clippy::type_complexity` even inside `#[test]` functions. The cheapest fix is a local type alias: `type WalkerFn = fn(&mut ActionEnvelope);` followed by `let walkers: [(WalkerFn, ActionPhase); 3] = ...`. Avoid `#[allow(clippy::type_complexity)]` for one-off tuples — the alias documents intent better and survives the next refactor.
reusable_lessons.clippy_doc_markdown_for_serde_json: Every PascalCase identifier in a doc comment must be backticked under `clippy::doc_markdown`, including casual mentions like `serde_json` (no underscore!) and `Number::F64`. Spec terms in normal prose (`RFC 8785`, `JCS`, `ECMA-262`) do NOT trigger it; the rule fires on identifier-shaped words only.
reusable_lessons.fixture_to_envelope_canonical_check: When validating that a hand-written fixture's structure matches the Rust envelope shape, do a *both-paths* canonical comparison: (1) parse fixture as `serde_json::Value`, JCS-canonicalize → `raw_canon`; (2) parse fixture as `ActionEnvelope`, JCS-canonicalize → `env_canon`; assert `raw_canon == env_canon`. If a fixture field is unknown to the struct, serde silently drops it, the env_canon string will be shorter, and the assertion will fail with a clear diff. This is cheaper than maintaining a denylist of fields.
